Who I Am

I'm a final-year B.Tech Computer Engineering student at NMIMS Mumbai, passionate about building technology that creates real-world impact. I have a strong end-to-end understanding of how software is built, tested, and shipped — spanning AI, full-stack development, and quality assurance.

From a multimodal AI system for tuberculosis detection to microservices-based clinical platforms and NLP-powered learning tools, I thrive on solving complex problems. I believe in learning continuously, building iteratively, and improving relentlessly.

Research Publications
Contributions to academic research
Under Review — 2026
Multimodal AI for Tuberculosis Detection through Fusion of Radiographic and Acoustic Biomarkers
Aakash Myana et al.  ·  2026  ·  Manuscript Under Review
This research presents a novel multimodal AI system that fuses chest X-ray radiographic imaging with cough acoustic biomarkers for early-stage tuberculosis detection. Leveraging CNN-based deep learning for imaging and spectral feature analysis for audio signals, the system achieves approximately 92% detection accuracy — outperforming single-modality approaches. This work aims to enable more accessible and accurate TB screening, particularly in resource-limited healthcare settings.
Deep LearningMedical ImagingMultimodal AITB DetectionAcoustic BiomarkersCNNTensorFlow
More research in progress. Stay tuned!
